methods.description: Numerical simulations based on the Beam Propagation Method (BPM) to study how the valve interacts with an incoming plane wave at different wavelengths.

steps.description: SEM images of the inner and outer surface of the C. wailesii valve have been converted into binary refractive-index maps.
steps.searchable_summary: convert SEM images to binary refractive-index maps

steps.description: The maps have been properly extruded and positioned to obtain the bilayer geometry characteristic of the valve.
steps.searchable_summary: extrude and position refractive-index maps for bilayer geometry

steps.description: The incoming fields were launched along the Z axis and the valve extends from z = 0 μm to z = 1 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate incoming fields along Z axis

steps.description: The output of the simulation is the spatial distribution of the transmitted intensity in XZ and YZ planes.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze spatial distribution of transmitted intensity
outputs: intensity distribution in XZ and YZ planes

methods.description: Acquisition of Raman spectra using a homebuilt Raman microscope to analyze diatom valves.

steps.description: A laser beam at 532 nm was spatially filtered, expanded, and introduced in an inverted microscope to illuminate a single diatom valve.
steps.searchable_summary: setup laser beam for Raman spectroscopy

steps.description: The back-scattered light from the sample was collected and filtered to eliminate residual Rayleigh scattering.
The Raman light was focused onto the entrance slit of a monochromator and detected by a CCD.
steps.searchable_summary: focus Raman light onto monochromator and detect with CCD

steps.description: 25 spectra were randomly collected from a single diatom with a step size of 10 μm and an exposure time of 10 s per spectrum.
steps.searchable_summary: collect multiple Raman spectra from diatom

methods.description: Numerical simulations using the Beam Propagation Method (BPM) corrected for wide angles to model light propagation through diatom valves.

steps.description: Starting from the Helmoltz equation, the electric field is separated into a slowly varying envelope factor and a rapid varying phase factor.
steps.searchable_summary: separate electric field into envelope and phase factors

steps.description: Using the slowly varying envelope approximation, derive the basic BPM equation.
steps.searchable_summary: derive BPM equation using envelope approximation

steps.description: Iterate U along the z-axis using finite differences for the x and y derivatives.
steps.searchable_summary: iterate U along z-axis using finite differences

steps.description: Use Padé approximations to derive a wide-angle BPM equation.
steps.searchable_summary: derive wide-angle BPM equation using Padé approximations

methods.description: Acquisition of steady-state photoluminescence (PL) spectra from diatom valves using a He-Cd laser.

steps.description: PL spectra were excited by a continuous wave He-Cd laser at 325 and 442 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: excite PL spectra using He-Cd laser

steps.description: PL was collected at normal incidence to the surface of samples through a fiber, then dispersed by a spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and disperse PL using spectrometer

steps.description: Detected using a Peltier cooled CCD camera.
steps.searchable_summary: detect PL spectra using CCD camera
